name: Agent Economics
core_question: How should resources be allocated in a federation where no central planner exists?
description: Resource allocation, cost of computation, budget constraints, multi-LLM failover economics, API cost optimization. The economy of cognitive agents.
human_world_bridge: Agent economics directly mirrors resource allocation problems in human economies. How do you distribute scarce resources (API calls, compute, attention) across competing needs without a central planner? The same question applies to healthcare budgets, energy grids, and public goods.
research_axes: Cognitive redundancy under budget constraints (multi-LLM failover cost)
Cost-benefit analysis of research depth vs. speed
Tragedy of the commons in shared federation infrastructure
